mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which historically precedes corporate earnings downgrades and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth accelerates concurrently with industrial production expansion, it confirms synchronized demand-supply alignment that boosts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 210, persistent inflation pressures force monetary policy to remain restrictive, compressing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral despite CPI readings above 210, or the forward return differential between high-CPI and low-CPI regimes shows no statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic momentum that supports corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Rank IC for PAYEMS changes falls below 0.02, or 5-day forward returns show no directional relationship with payroll growth trends.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 1300 thousand units signals weakening construction demand and consumer confidence, which historically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as market participants price in slower economic activity.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when housing starts breach the 1300 threshold, or the signal's out-of-sample accuracy drops below 55% over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and declining residential mortgage debt outstanding indicates tightening financial conditions that restrict household spending and corporate investment, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-RSAFS signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 115, robust manufacturing and utility output confirms strong aggregate supply capacity that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO breaches 115, or the signal's predictive power reverses direction during supply chain disruption periods.
